SAP S/4HANA - Procurement

EWM100

Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) - Basics

S4C03

SAP S/4HANA - Customizing für Vertrieb

S4F10

SAP S/4HANA - Financial Management

S4C01

SAP S/4HANA - Sales and Distribution

HA400

SAP HANA - Administration

CLD200

SAP Cloud Platform - Development

EWM110

Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) - Advanced

THR80

SAP SuccessFactors - Performance Management

BC400

ABAP Programming for SAP S/4HANA

Eine Übersicht über alle SAP Schulungssysteme ist hier zu finden.

 

Kostenfreie SAP Learning Journeys

SAP bietet kostenfreie Learning Journeys an, die Text, Video und Quizzes beinhalten und ideal für Selbstlerner sind, um sich in ein Thema einzuarbeiten. Sie haben die openSAP Kurse abgelöst und bieten eine einfache kostenfreie Möglichkeit zum Lernen. Anbei die Übersicht über die beliebtesten im Jahr 2024.

  • Discovering SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Acquiring Core ABAP Skills
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Exploring SAP Cloud ERP
  • Managing Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Outlining the Financial Accounting Overview in SAP S/4HANA
  • Learning the Basics of SAP Fiori
  • Exploring SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Practicing Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
  • Developing with SAP Integration Suite
  • Learning the Basics of ABAP Programming on SAP BTP
  • Exploring the SAP Business AI Portfolio
  • Getting Started with Support from SAP (Support Accreditation)
  • Positioning the SAP Business Technology Platform
  • Explore Integrated Business Processes in SAP S/4HANA
  • Discovering End-to-End Business Processes for the Intelligent Enterprise
  • Discovering SAP Activate - Implementation Tools and Methodology
  • Managing Business Processes with SAP Signavio Solutions

Ich hoffe, dass ich damit einige Inspirationen geben konnte – probiert gerne das eine oder andere aus.

Wer Up-To-Date bleiben möchte, abonniert am besten den monatlichen SAP Training Newsletter. Dort werden regelmäßig Neuigkeiten rund um SAP Lernangebote jeglicher Methodik geteilt.

Viel Spaß beim Lernen!

entry.title: Study Guide for SAP Certified Associate - Business User - SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Exam 2411
entry.content:
Abstract

This study guide is designed to aid in your preparation for the SAP Certified Associate - Business User - SAP Commerce Cloud exam (C_C4H32_2411). This certification is intended for professionals who possess fundamental and core knowledge in SAP Commerce Cloud and can function effectively as part of a project team under guidance. It is ideal for business users seeking an entry-level qualification to work with SAP Commerce Cloud.

If you are preparing for the SAP Certified Professional - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud exam (P_C4H34_2411), please check out the other dedicated study guide.

In addition: related certification preparation live sessions are also scheduled in Q1 2025, check the information in this blog post.

Overview

Before we begin, it's important to note that proper preparation for the exam is highly recommended. To ensure success, consider attending the following:

  • C4H320 - SAP Commerce Cloud Business User Training: This course contains ALL the essential content required for passing the exam. Enrolling in this course is highly beneficial.
  • Hands-on Practice System for C4H320: If attending the C4H320 training is not feasible, you can independently book this system. It offers practice opportunities with all the hands-on exercises from the training and provides access to all the training material PDFs.
  • Business User Certification Preparation Live Session: This live session features an instructor who reviews key exam topics and can directly address many of your related questions.

This exam covers the following 6 topic areas:

  • Product Content Management
  • Web Content Management
  • Commerce Management
  • Order Management & Customer Support
  • Integrations
  • Essential Foundation

Let's explore each exam topic area and its essential checkpoints in detail.

All Topics Walk-Through

1. Product Content Management (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should aim to understand the following:

  • Backoffice Product Cockpit Features: Gain a comprehensive understanding of all views (list/grid), functionalities, and toolbar actions, with particular emphasis on synchronization, Excel import/export, and cloning.
  • Product Concepts: Familiarize yourself with products, categories, catalogs, and catalog versions. Assess catalog management involving single or multiple catalog versions and understand performance characteristics.
  • Product Modeling Approaches: Explore type systems, basic and multi-dimensional variants, and classification systems.
  • Media Management: Focus on media containers and media formats, as well as conversion and storage processes.

The essentials links includes:

  • Backoffice Product Content Management - User Guide
  • Working with Catalogs
  • Product Modelling
  • Synchronizing Catalogs
  • Classification
  • Modeling Product Variants
  • B2B Multi-Dimensional Products
  • Digital Asset Management
  • Media Conversion

2. Web Content Management (11-20%)

In this topic area, focus on understanding the following:

  • Concepts of Base Stores and Related Items: Grasp the relationships among base stores, sites, product catalogs, and content catalogs.
  • CMS Components: Explore page templates, content slots, and CMS restrictions.
  • SmartEdit Features: Become familiar with various views such as pages, navigation, and homepage, along with the modes (preview | basic edit | advanced edit | versioning) for editing and versioning features.
  • Personalization Concepts: Understand segments, triggers, actions, and the configuration of personalization modules via SmartEdit and Backoffice.
  • Commerce Customization: Learn principles and applications of personalized search and promotions.
  • Composable Storefront: Understand the working principles and the communication process between composable storefront and SAP Commerce Cloud.

The essentials links are:

  • Overview of the CMS2 Data Model
  • Setting Up a Basic Web Site in the Backoffice
  • SmartEdit Features
  • Personalization Module Features
  • SAP Commerce Cloud, Composable Storefront Documentation

3. Commerce Management (11-20%)

In this topic area, your should develop a thorough understanding of the following:

  • Cart and Checkout Management: Familiarize yourself with the cart handling process and the various types of checkout options available.
  • Pricing Mechanism and Functionalities: Delve into the aspects of a price factory, price rows, and the evaluation priorities associated with multiple price rows linked to the same product.
  • Search Management: Understand the integration with the default search engine, focusing on index management, search box configuration, facets configuration, and the Adaptive Search perspective in Backoffice (including search profiles, activation sets, promoting items and boost rules, etc).
  • Promotion and Coupon Management: Learn about the setup and configuration of promotions, including conditions and actions, the evaluation process, the distinctions between single-code and multi-code coupons, and the role of coupons in promotions.
  • B2B Commerce Models: Gain insight into typical B2B data models, such as B2B users, units, cost centers, budgets, and credit limits. Also, understand B2B process support, including order approval with B2B permissions, quote negotiations, and punchout integration processes.

Here are the important links:

  • Search and Navigation Module Features
  • Adaptive Search Module Features
  • Promotion Engine Features
  • Coupon Features
  • Price Calculation
  • B2B Commerce Features
  • B2B PunchOut Module

4. Order Management and Customer Support (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should focus on:

  • Payment Integration: Understand SOP (Silent Order Post) and HOP (Hosted Order Page)
  • Basic Order Management Extensions: Explore functions such as standard order fulfillment processes, order replenishment, order splitting, and stock information management.
  • Order Management Services Module: Understand the configuration of various aspects, including Available to Promise (ATP), sourcing, and the default fulfillment workflow involving picking, packing, labeling, and shipment.
  • Assisted Service and Customer Service Modules: Learn about the features of the Assisted Service Module (such as creating customer profiles, managing shopping carts, and placing/canceling/returning orders) for storefronts, and the Customer Service Module (including managing tickets, approving reviews, and generating GDPR reports) in Backoffice.
  • Fraud Detection: Grasp the principles of the "game of points" system and the default symptom types used in fraud detection.

The important links are:

  • Base Commerce Module Features (Order Related Ones)
  • Order Management Features
  • Assisted Service Module Features
  • Customer Service Module Features

5. Integrations (<10%)

In this topic area, you should understand the following:

  • SAP Integration Pillars: Focus on the integration of data, process, and feature aspects.
  • Integration Scenarios: Examine different setups such as loosely coupled, pick-and-choose, and tightly coupled configurations.
  • General Integration Tools: Learn about various tools and services, including Impex, Cloud Hotfolder, OCC web services, integration API module, and Webhooks.
  • Integration Extension Pack: Identify the default support available for SAP solutions and systems.
  • SAP BTP Integration Suite and Kyma Runtime: Explore their features and underlying concepts.

More information in:

  • Introduction to SAP Commerce Integrations
  • Integration API Features
  • Cloud Hot Folders
  • SAP Commerce Cloud, Integration Extension Pack
  • SAP Back-End Integration Overview

6. Essential Foundations (21-30%)

In this topic area, you should understand the following:

  • Concepts of Extensions and Modules: Familiarize yourself with how extensions and modules function within the system.
  • SAP Commerce Cloud Architecture and Components: Gain insight into the structure and individual components of SAP Commerce Cloud.
  • License Management: Understand the nuances of using/managing temporary and production licenses.
  • Monthly Update Scheme**: Learn about the versioning system and the purpose, release process, and contents of each update, and how to stay current and supported.
  • SAP Commerce Cloud in the Public Cloud: Explore the differences and benefits compared to on-premises solutions, including environment setup and scaling support.
  • Backoffice General Functionalities: Get to know the general functions and GUI components, such as the top menu bar.
  • Management of the Validation Framework via Backoffice: Understand how to manage validation rules and processes.
  • Cronjobs: Learn about the definition, application, and execution of cronjobs.
  • Impex and Distributed ImpEx: Understand the definition, application, and execution of Impex, along with the concept and advantages of distributed ImpEx.
  • Workflows and Collaboration Center: Learn how to define, manage, and monitor workflows within Backoffice.
  • Security Management:
    • User Management (principals, user groups)
    • Type-based Access Rights
    • Restrictions and Personalization Rules
    • HAC Access Customization
    • Password Policies and Change Audits
  • GDPR Support and Components: Understand the features and components that ensure GDPR compliance.
  • Intelligent Selling Services: identify the basic features and integration with SAP Commerce Cloud, especially the types of product recommendations provided.
  • Out-of-the-Box Features of B2C and B2B in Composable Storefront**: Familiarize yourself with the ready-to-use features available for B2C/B2B scenarios.

Here are the essential links:

  • Data Management with ImpEx
  • The Cronjob Service
  • Data Validation Framework
  • Workflow and Collaboration
  • Access Rights
  • Restrictions
  • SAP Commerce Cloud Architecture
  • Environment Components
  • License
  • Supported Releases
  • Update Releases
  • Intelligent Selling Services
     

Summary

By thoroughly exploring the above topic areas and the included links, you'll be well-prepared for the SAP Certified Associate - Business User - SAP Commerce Cloud exam.

Good luck on your certification journey, and may your hard work pay off!

entry.title: Study Guide for SAP Certified Professional - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Exam 2411
entry.content:
Abstract

This study guide is designed to assist you in preparing for the SAP Certified Professional - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud exam (P_C4H34_2411). This certification verifies that you possess the proficiency needed to work effectively with the SAP Commerce Cloud Suite. Catering to a professional/expert level audience, this certification demands candidates demonstrate technical skills and a deep understanding of SAP Commerce Cloud functionalities. With three years of practical experience being a prerequisite, the certification addresses complex problem-solving scenarios.

If you are preparing for the SAP Certified Associate - Business User - SAP Commerce Cloud exam (C_C4H32_2411), please check out this other dedicated study guide.

In addition: related certification preparation live sessions are also scheduled in Q1 2025, check the information in this blog post.

Before we start

To prepare for the certification, it's highly recommended to engage in comprehensive training sessions and/or online learning platforms. The below options provide structured learning paths to develop the necessary skills:

  • Instructor-led Training:
    • C4H340 - SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 1
    • C4H341 - SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 2
  • E-Learning Courses: For those unable to attend training sessions in person, e-learning options are available with equivalent content.
    • Beginner: Exploring the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
    • Intermediate: Expand Upon Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
    • Advanced: Mastering the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
  • Hands-on Practice Systems: These trainings and e-learning offerings are complemented by dedicated practice systems, allowing you to perform hands-on exercises with SAP Commerce Cloud. This practical experience is designed to enhance your understanding, with PDFs of the training materials accessible in the exercise environments. Specifically:
    • The following practice systems correspond to the mentioned trainings:
      • Hands-on Practice for SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 1
      • Hands-on Practice for SAP Commerce Cloud Developer Part 2
    • Additionally, the practice systems related to the three e-learning courses include:
      • Hands-on Practice for Exploring the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
      • Hands-on Practice for Expanding Upon SAP Commerce Cloud Technical Essentials
      • Hands-on Practice for Mastering the Technical Essentials of SAP Commerce Cloud
         
  • Certification Live Session - SAP Commerce Cloud - Developer Certification Preparation: This live session features an instructor who reviews key exam topics and can directly address many of your related questions.

But please note: These resources provide a comprehensive foundation but aren't exhaustive for examination readiness due to the certification's professional level.

The certification exam is categorized into the 10 topic areas outlined below:

  • Platform Basics
  • Platform Features
  • PCM (Product Content Management)
  • Commerce
  • Customer Experience
  • Order Management and Customer Services
  • Commerce Cloud and Cloud Portal
  • Backoffice Framework
  • Integration
  • Managing Clean Core

Let's explore each topic area in detail.

Walkthrough of All Topics

1. Platform Basics (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should focus on the following objectives:

  • Comprehend the Ant build framework and essential configuration files.
  • Explain the concept of extensions and manage them effectively, including property files and dependencies.
  • Describe data modeling concepts and the purpose of 'items.xml', including item types, primary key generation, relationships/collections, enumerations, maps, dynamic attributes, and database type deployment.
  • Manage security aspects related to type-based access rights and restrictions.
  • Master writing flexible search queries.
  • Understand ImpEx syntax, how to execute ImpEx, and its scripting support.
  • Identify the features and implementation of service-oriented architecture and the service layer, including model objects, monitoring via interceptors, and management using 'modelService'.

Important Links:

  • Building SAP Commerce
  • Extension Concept
  • Type System
  • ServiceLayer
  • Users in the SAP Commerce Platform
  • Search restrictions
  • Spring Framework in SAP Commerce
  • Search Mechanisms
  • Data Management with ImpEx
  • ImpEx API

2. Platform Features (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should aim to:

  • Understand the event framework, including special event types like 'ClusterAwareEvent', 'TransactionAwareEvent', and 'AfterSaveEvent'.
  • Manage CronJobs by working with data models, implementation steps, and aborting methods.
  • Identify caching features, such as the framework, eviction policies, configuration options, and clustering.
  • Explain the data conversion and population process.
  • Import data using the system setup utility and patch classes.
  • Understand the implementation and customization of Omni Commerce Connect (OCC).
  • Identify the functionalities of workflows, workflow templates, and the collaboration center.
  • Understand scripting support and its applications.

Important Links:

  • Event System
  • The Cron Job Service
  • Cache
  • Converters and Populators
  • Workflow and Collaboration
  • Omni Commerce Connect
  • Scripting Engine

3. PCM (Product Content Management) (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should aim to understand the following:

  • Product and catalog management, including products, categories, catalog versions, and synchronization management.
  • Different product modeling approaches, such as basic variants, multi-dimensional variants, and the classification system.
  • Media management, focusing on elements like media containers, media formats, and synchronization.

Important Links:

  • Product Modelling
  • Classification
  • Working with Catalogs
  • Synchronizing Catalogs
  • Media
  • Muiti-Dimensional variants

4. Commerce (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Understand the search and navigation module, encompassing backoffice configuration, indexed types, index queries/jobs/operations, indexed properties, facet and facet range configuration, as well as search services and listeners.
  • Identify the features of the adaptive search module, including its configuration, search profiles, and the boosting of items and rules.
  • Manage and customize the rule engine-based promotion module, covering out-of-the-box promotions, conditions and actions, rule-aware objects, and coupon types.
  • Explain the pricing mechanism, focusing on price rows and their decoupling from catalogs.
  • Understand basic stock management, including data models, the stock service, and display on storefronts.
  • Identify cart management and the implementation of the commerce cart service.
  • Recognize the relationship between BaseStore, Catalogs, and Warehouses.

Important Links:

  • Search And Navigation Module
  • Adaptive Search Module
  • Promotion
  • Price, Tax, and Discount Calculation
  • Commerce Service Module

5. Customer Experience (11-20%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Identify the out-of-the-box CMS item types, including base store, base site, template, page, content slot, and component, as well as their type hierarchy.
  • Implement and manage custom CMS component types and CMS restrictions.
  • Recognize the key features of SAP Commerce Cloud, composable storefront.
  • Understand the features and management of CMS content using SmartEdit, including various modes such as preview, basic/advanced edit, versioning, and navigation management.
  • Comprehend the personalization module, including related data models, calculation processes, voters, and commerce customization for personalized search and promotions.

Important Links:

  • SmartEdit
  • WCMS Module
  • Personalization module
  • SAP Commerce Cloud, composable storefront

6. Order Management and Customer Services (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Understand the basecommerce module and typical Order APIs, including splitting strategies, cancellations, returns, and refunds.
  • Manage existing and custom business processes, focusing on their concepts, node types, and custom implementation.
  • Identify the standard checkout and fulfillment processes.
  • Describe the features of the Assisted Service Module and Customer Service Module.
  • Understand the Order Management Services Module.

Important Links:

  • Base Commerce Module
  • Order Management Services Module
  • Assisted Service Module
  • Customer Service Module
  • Business Process Management

7. Commerce Cloud and Cloud Portal (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Explain the architecture of SAP Commerce Cloud.
  • Understand how to set up the code repository.
  • Describe how to configure 'manifest.json' files, manage environments, and configure endpoints.
  • Explain how to connect to the code repository and how to build and deploy your application on the Cloud Portal.

Important Links:

  • Build Manifest Components
  • Cloud Portal
  • Cloud Automation Components
  • Project Customizations

8. Backoffice Framework (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Describe how to create and manage backoffice applications using the Backoffice framework.
    • This includes managing widgets, configuring connections, utilizing the application orchestrator, implementing dynamic forms, and using virtual sockets, among other features.

Important Links:

  • Backoffice Framework Module, especially:
    • Widgets
    • UI Configuration Overview
    • Dynamic Forms in UI Configuration
    • Virtual Sockets
  • Hands-on Practice for SAP Commerce Cloud Backoffice Framework Developer

9. Integration (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Explain how to apply, manage, and monitor Integration APIs and Webhooks within the Backoffice.
  • Identify the features and functionalities of Cloud Hot Folders.
  • Recognize the basic features of Intelligent Selling Services and the SAP CX AI Toolkit for SAP Commerce Cloud.

Important Links:

  • Integration API Module
  • Cloud Hot Folders
  • Webhooks
  • Intelligent Selling Services
  • SAP CX AI Toolkit

10. Managing Clean Core (<=10%)

In this topic area, you should:

  • Understand the concept of Clean Core and its essential components.
  • Identify the application of Clean Core across various dimensions, including Business Processes, Extensions, Data, Landscapes, and Operations.

Important Links:

  • Managing Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, especially:
    • Exploring How to Make Landscapes Core Compliant
    • Exploring How to Make Operations Core Compliant

Summary

This study guide sets a strong foundation by outlining crucial topic areas to focus on along with recommended learning resources. Equip yourself with both theoretical knowledge and practical insight to excel in the certification exam.

Happy learning and good luck with your preparation!

entry.title: Conquering the SAP BTP Solution Architect Certification: A Preparation Guide
entry.content:
This blog post provides a comprehensive guide to help you effectively prepare for the P_BTPA_2408 certification:

1. Understand the Exam Blueprint:

  • Thoroughly review the official exam blueprint. This document outlines the exam objectives, topics, and weighting.

    Exam code : P_BTPA_2408
    Questions: 40 (single and multiple choice )
    Time: 3 hours
    Cut-off: 68%

    visit the link for more information 

    https://learning.sap.com/certifications/sap-certified-professional-solution-architect-sap-btp

    • Focus on key areas: Pay close attention to areas like:
      • Core BTP Services: Deep dive into services like SAP Integration Suite, SAP Data Intelligence , SAP BTP Available environment, SAP AI Core,destination/Connectivity,API management ,Cloud Identity service 
      • Architecture and Design Principles: Understand BTP's architecture, design patterns, and best practices for building robust and scalable solutions.All Architecture(Data/Integration/Extension ) methodology explained in the course 
      • Account model : Learn about available account model in BTP
      • Integration Scenarios: Explore various integration scenarios, including on-premise to cloud, cloud-to-cloud, and B2B integrations.
      • Development options available: Understanding of all available option for LCNC ,Build family comparison ,AI Core service

2. Leverage Official SAP Resources:

  • SAP Learning Hub: Utilize the SAP Learning Hub for access courses, tutorials, and practice exams.

    https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/becoming-an-sap-btp-solution-architect

    If you go through available course content at learning hub,it will provide you a solid root for exam preparation

  • SAP Help Portal: Explore the extensive documentation and help resources available on the SAP Help Portal for go deeper on BTP available service
  • SAP Discovery center https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/index.html 
  •  SAP Business Accelerator Hub : https://api.sap.com/
  • SAP Community: Engage with the SAP Community forums to connect with other professionals, ask questions, and share knowledge.

3. Hands-on Experience:

  • Gain practical experience: Work on hands-on exercises, labs, and projects to solidify your understanding of BTP concepts.

    You can explore available BTP trail account to explore various service and other development/security related options

    https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/hcp-create-trial-account..html

    • Build sample applications: Develop simple applications using BTP services to gain practical experience and reinforce your learning.
    • Explore real-world scenarios: Analyze real-world business challenges and design BTP-based solutions to address them,As in the exam there are many questions on real world scenarios and you have to select the best possible answer for that.

4. Study and Practice Consistently:

  • Create a study plan: Allocate dedicated time for consistent study and review.
  • Use flashcards and mind maps: To summarize key concepts and aid in memorization.Specially to understand different methodology and available Build options
  • Review and revise: Regularly review previously studied material to reinforce your understanding.

5. Seek Guidance and Support:

  • Connect with mentors or experienced SAP professionals: Seek guidance from experienced colleagues or mentors who have successfully passed the certification.
  • Join study groups: Collaborate with other candidates to share knowledge, discuss challenging topics, and stay motivated.

6. Exam Day Tips:

  • Read each question carefully: Ensure you understand the question and all its parts before answering.
  • Time: Available time is more than sufficient for the exam
  • Test Readiness : After booking the exam please check the exam guideline and preparation instructions carefully.
  • Review your answers: You can mark questions for review during the exam and review your answers before submitting the exam.

7. Conclusion:

The P_BTPA_2408 certification requires dedication and effort. By following these guidelines and consistently working towards your goals, you can increase your chances of success and gain valuable expertise in the SAP BTP ecosystem.

Disclaimer: This blog post provides general guidance and should not be considered an official or guaranteed method for exam success. Always refer to the official SAP documentation and resources for the most up-to-date information.

I hope this blog post helps you in your preparation for the SAP BTP Solution Architect certification!

HappY Learning 

Thanks & Best Regards,

Atul Kumar Jain

entry.title: Elevate Your SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Journey with Our Certification Live Sessions
entry.content:
Abstract

In the ever-evolving world of SAP Commerce Cloud, achieving success means staying up-to-date with the latest features and insights. In response to the updated certification exams (2411) for business and technical users at the end of 2024, we are excited to offer two comprehensive certification preparation live sessions in Q1 2025. These sessions are designed to equip you with the tools and knowledge needed to excel in the crucial SAP Commerce Cloud certification exams.

Please be aware that any certification live session recordings you might encounter from before 2025 are outdated in relation to the current certification exams.

Live Sessions Overview

Live Session 1 (1 hour): Certification Prep for SAP Commerce Cloud – Business User

Purpose: This session is tailored to support participants preparing for the SAP Certified Application Associate - SAP Commerce Cloud Business User exam.

Certification Level: Associate

Target Audience: Business Consultants, Technology Consultants, Software Architects, and anyone interested in taking the certification exam.

Agenda:

  • Essential Topics Focus: Concentrate on key subjects from the Business User Certification Exam, emphasizing critical components that will be evaluated.
  • Strategic Preparation Guidance: Receive expert advice on crafting an effective study plan for associate-level success.

Live Session 2 (1 hour): Certification Prep for SAP Commerce Cloud - Developer

Purpose: This session is dedicated to empowering participants for the SAP Certified Development Professional - SAP Commerce Cloud Developer exam.

Certification Level: Professional

Target Audience: Software Developers, Software Architects, Technology Consultants, and anyone interested in taking the certification exam.

Agenda:

  • Key Topics Exploration: Dive into the main topics of the Developer Certification Exam, highlighting crucial elements that will be assessed.
  • Expert Preparation Tips: Gain insights into creating a robust study plan for professional-level certification success.

Common Elements Across Both Sessions

  • Comprehensive Coverage: Each session will systematically explore the exam topics, ensuring thorough understanding of essential components.
  • Interactive Learning: Engage with the content through interactive sessions, enriched by instructors' expertise and collective group insights.

Preferred Written Learning Materials?

If you prefer written guides, explore our study guides for both Business User and Developer exams to complement your preparation:

  • Study Guide for SAP Certified Associate - Business User - SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Exam 2411
  • Study Guide for SAP Certified Professional - Developer - SAP Commerce Cloud Certification Exam 2411

Already Certified?

If you've already achieved SAP Commerce Cloud certification in 2024 or before, remember to renew it by retaking the updated exam or participating in the stay certified programs. Look for email notifications from SAP about these programs and consider the following courses as preparation:

  1. Stay Certified – SAP Commerce Cloud Business User
  2. Stay Certified – SAP Commerce Cloud Developer

Conclusion

These upcoming live sessions in Q1 2025 are designed to be pivotal in your SAP Commerce Cloud certification journey. Whether you're a budding developer or a seasoned business user, expect in-depth coverage of essential exam topics and strategic preparation advice. This is about more than just passing exams - it's about becoming a leader in the SAP Commerce Cloud ecosystem.

Thanks for reading, and we look forward to seeing you at the sessions!

entry.title: Operational Services: Partner mit zertifizierter SAP-Kompetenz und umfassendem Security-Portfolio
entry.content:
Unser Partner operational services GmbH & Co. KG wurde im Dezember 2024 zum 10. Mal in Folge als SAP Operations Partner für seine Services zertifiziert und dafür mit einem goldenen Zertifikat ausgezeichnet. Zudem haben sie sich erstmalig für Zertifizierung SAP security operations entschieden. Wir schätzen die langjährige Zusammenarbeit mit operational services sehr und freuen uns, dass sie bereit waren, ihre Erfahrungen als SAP-zertifizierter Operations-Partner mit uns zu teilen.

Würden Sie uns zu Beginn kurz Ihr Unternehmen vorstellen und dabei insbesondere auf die von Ihnen angebotenen Services eingehen?

Wir als operational services GmbH & Co. KG sind einer der führenden ICT Service Provider im deutschen Markt. Als eigenständiges Unternehmen und Tochtergesellschaft der T-Systems International GmbH unterstützen wir unsere Kunden mit maßgeschneiderten ICT-Services – von der Beratung über den ganzheitlichen Betrieb von Rechenzentren bis zu vorkonfigurierten ICT-Lösungen. Mit unseren rund 1.200 hochqualifizierten Mitarbeiterinnen und Mitarbeitern entwickeln und betreiben wir für unsere Kunden modernste Informationssysteme und sichern den langfristigen Systemsupport sowie die Verfügbarkeit kritischer Betriebsprozesse ab. Wir begleiten unsere Kunden mit IT-Lösungen & Services auf ihrem individuellen Weg der digitalen Transformation.  

Übergabe des goldenen Zertifikats im Dezember 2024

 

Was hat Sie dazu bewogen, im Laufe der Zeit weitere Services zertifizieren zu lassen?

Die wachsende und sich stetig verändernde Produktpalette der SAP ist natürlich auch für uns Ansporn, diesen Services im Betrieb und im Consulting gerecht zu werden und unseren Kunden zertifiziert anbieten zu können. Das waren neben dem Wandel von SAP ERP/ECC zu SAP S/4HANA auch die begleitende Cloud-Journey sowie die Ergänzungen im Portfolio der SAP.

Sie haben gerade auch erstmalig die Zertifizierung für SAP security operations erhalten. Welche Bedeutung hat dieses Thema für OS und seine Kunden?

Die steigende Bedrohung der IT durch Sicherheitslücken ist derzeit omnipräsent. Wir freuen uns, dass wir unseren Anspruch an die Sicherheit der Daten unserer Kunden nun auch im Bereich SAP Operations erfolgreich unter Beweis stellen konnten. Viele Services und Maßnahmen aus unserer Security-Baseline und dem darauf aufbauenden Portfolio bilden den Grundstein für diesen Erfolg.

Welche spezifischen Dienstleistungen bieten Sie in den Bereichen Sicherheit und Cloud-Migration Ihren Kunden an?

Neben den Standard-Sicherheitsdiensten bieten wir ein breites Security-Portfolio an. Angefangen bei maßgeschneiderter Security-Beratung über Penetrationstests und SIEM/SOC-Services bis hin zu Software-Testing, Vulnerability Management und Audits. Damit decken wir alle Aspekte von IT-Sicherheit ab, um ein sicheres digitales Umfeld für unsere Kunden zu schaffen.

 

 

Informationen zum SAP Zertifizierungsprogramm für Operations Partner und eine Übersicht über alle zertifizierten Partner finden Sie auf unserem SAP Operations Partner Guide.

entry.title: SAP Sales Cloud Stay Certified update
entry.content:
On behalf of the CRM & CX Product Learning CoE team, I am pleased to announce the release of our updated SAP Sales Cloud Stay Certified Program.

Note: This is for the SAP Sales Cloud product (aka Cloud for Customer) and not SAP Sales Cloud Version 2

The  SAP Certified Associate - Implementation Consultant - SAP Sales Cloud confirms your proficiency in the core functional and administrative aspects of SAP Sales Cloud, which are essential for the consultant role. This certification demonstrates your comprehensive understanding and advanced technical skills necessary to contribute effectively as a member of an SAP Sales Cloud implementation project team. It is highly recommended as an entry-level certification for this product.

Upon successfully passing this certification, consultants have the opportunity to extend the validity of their certification by an additional year through the Stay Current program, which involves taking an assessment to ensure their skills and knowledge remain up to date with the latest SAP Sales Cloud innovations and best practices.

Our Stay Certified program consists of:

  • Stay Certified SAP Sales Cloud An online course where you can learn about the major enhancements in the product as of the 2408 release to prepare for the assessment to stay certified.
  • An assessment that evaluates your understanding of the latest features. It can be accessed from the ‘My Learning’ area of learning.sap.com

Note: The Stay Certified offer is for subscribed learners on learning.sap.com platform.

entry.title: Live Sessions im SAP Learning Hub: Interaktiv SAP lernen
entry.content:
Was sind Live Sessions im SAP Learning Hub?

Live Sessions sind Online-Veranstaltungen, die in der Regel ein bis zwei Stunden dauern und von SAP-Experten geleitet werden. Sie finden das ganze Jahr über statt und decken ein breites Spektrum an SAP-Themen ab. Im Jahr 2024 haben über 200.000 Lernende dieses Angebot genutzt. Um möglichst viele Teilnehmende weltweit zu erreichen, werden die Sessions in zwei Zeitzonen und in englischer Sprache angeboten.

Die Live Sessions haben verschiedene Formate. Sie sind zu einem Großteil

  • Expert Deep Dives zu bestimmten SAP Themen.

Darüber hinaus gibt es

  • What’s New Sessions,
  • Stay Certified Sessions und
  • Get Started Sessions.

Gerade die Deep Dives und die Get Started Sessions haben eine auf Interaktion und Kollaboration ausgerichtetes Set up, das eine gute Interaktion zwischen Lernenden und Experten ermöglicht.

Vorteile der Live-Sessions im SAP Learning Hub

Diese Live-Sessions bieten eine Vielzahl von Vorteilen:

  1. Expertenwissen: Aktuelles Expertenwissen: SAP-Fachleute präsentieren die neuesten Themen und Technologien. Dies gewährleistet, dass Teilnehmer stets über die aktuellsten Entwicklungen und Produkte informiert sind.
  2. Interaktivität: Teilnehmer können in Echtzeit Fragen stellen und Diskussionen führen. Dies fördert nicht nur das Verständnis in komplexe Sachverhalte, sondern ermöglicht auch den Austausch von Erfahrungen und Best Practices. Auch können sie von Fragen anderer lernen.
  3. Praxisorientierung: Die Sessions konzentrieren sich oft auf reale Anwendungsfälle. Dies hilft den Teilnehmenden, das Gelernte direkt in ihrem Arbeitsalltag umzusetzen.
  4. Networking: Teilnehmer haben die Möglichkeit, sich mit Gleichgesinnten aus der SAP-Community auszutauschen. Dies fördert den Wissensaustausch und kann zu wertvollen beruflichen Kontakten führen.
  5. Flexibilität: Die Sessions werden zu verschiedenen Zeiten angeboten, um unterschiedliche Zeitzonen und Arbeitszeiten zu berücksichtigen. Zudem sind viele Sessions auch als Aufzeichnung verfügbar.
  6. Kostengünstigkeit: Für Abonnenten des SAP Learning Hub sind die Live Sessions im Abonnement enthalten, was eine kosteneffiziente Möglichkeit zur kontinuierlichen Weiterbildung darstellt.
  7. Aktualität der Themen: Die Themen der Live Sessions spiegeln die aktuellen Trends und Entwicklungen in der SAP-Welt wider. In jüngster Zeit gab es beispielsweise Sessions zu: SAP S/4HANA Cloud - SAP Business Technology Platform - SAP Analytics Cloud - Integration verschiedener SAP-Lösungen und viele Sessions zu Artificial Intelligence.

Die Live Sessions im SAP Learning Hub ergänzen die on-demand Lerninhalte optimal und bieten SAP-Profis die Möglichkeit, ihr Wissen kontinuierlich zu erweitern und auf dem neuesten Stand zu bleiben. Sie sind ein wichtiger Baustein für die professionelle Entwicklung in der sich schnell wandelnden Welt der SAP-Technologien.

Für Interessierte empfiehlt es sich, regelmäßig das Angebot im SAP Learning Hub zu prüfen und relevante Live Sessions in die persönliche Weiterbildungsplanung einzubeziehen. Bei der Auswahl der relevanten Live Sessions helfen die Selektionsfilter wie Level (Vorkenntnisse) oder Rolle des Lernenden.

Die Kombination aus flexiblem Online-Lernen und interaktiven Live-Veranstaltungen macht den SAP Learning Hub zu einer umfassenden Ressource für alle, die in der SAP-Welt up-to-date bleiben möchten.

Das Live-Session-Angebot wird unter learning.sap.com veröffentlicht und kontinuierlich auf alle aktuellen Produktbereiche ausgeweitet. Hier finden Sie die die kommenden Live Sessions.

Für regelmäßige Updates zum Lernangebot der SAP empfehlen wir, den SAP Training Newsletter zu abonnieren.

Wir freuen uns über Fragen und Anmerkungen in den Kommentaren.

entry.title: Live Sessions in SAP Learning Hub: Interactive Learning with SAP
entry.content:
What are Live Sessions in  SAP Learning Hub? Live Sessions are live virtual events, typically lasting one to two hours, led by SAP experts. They take place throughout the year and cover a wide range of SAP topics. In 2024, more than 200,000 learners took advantage of this offering. To reach as many participants worldwide as possible, the sessions are offered in two time zones and in English.

Live Sessions come in various formats. The majority are:

  • Expert Deep Dives into specific SAP topics.

In addition, there are:

  • What’s New Sessions,
  • Stay Certified Sessions, and
  • Get Started Sessions.

Advantages of Live Sessions in  SAP Learning Hub Live Sessions offer a wide range of benefits:

  1. Expert Knowledge: Up-to-date expert knowledge is shared by SAP professionals, presenting the latest topics and technologies. This ensures that participants are always informed about the latest developments and products.
  2. Interactivity: Participants can ask questions in real time and engage in discussions. This not only promotes understanding of complex topics but also allows for the exchange of experiences and best practices. Learners can also benefit from the questions posed by others.
  3. Practical Relevance: The sessions often focus on real-life use cases. This helps participants apply what they’ve learned directly to their work.
  4. Networking: Participants have the opportunity to network with like-minded individuals from the SAP community. This encourages knowledge sharing and can lead to valuable professional connections.
  5. Flexibility: The sessions are offered at various times to accommodate different time zones and work hours. Some sessions are also recorded
  6. Cost-Effectiveness: For subscribers of SAP Learning Hub, Live Sessions are included in the subscription, providing a cost-effective way to continue learning.
  7. Relevance of Topics: Live session topics reflect the latest trends and developments in the SAP world. Recently, there have been sessions on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, SAP Business Technology Platform, SAP Analytics Cloud, integration of various SAP solutions, and many sessions on Artificial Intelligence.

Live Sessions in SAP Learning Hub complement the on-demand, self-paced learning content perfectly and offer SAP professionals the opportunity to continuously expand their knowledge and stay up-to-date. They are an important building block for professional development in the rapidly changing world of SAP technologies.

 

For those interested, it is recommended to regularly check the offerings in SAP Learning Hub and incorporate relevant Live Sessions into their personal learning plan. Selection filters, such as level (prior knowledge) or role of the learner, help when choosing relevant Live Sessions.
The combination of flexible online learning and interactive live events makes the SAP Learning Hub a comprehensive resource for anyone who wants to stay up-to-date in the SAP world.

Live Sessions are published on learning.sap.com and the schedule is continuously expanded to cover all current product areas. You can find upcoming Live Sessions here.
For regular updates on SAP’s learning offerings, we recommend subscribing to the SAP Training Newsletter.

We look forward to your questions and comments.
